q: Which statement about the carbon cycle is correct?
options: Combustion of fossil fuels introduces carbon that was previously cycling actively in the biosphere
Photosynthesis releases carbon dioxide while respiration consumes it
Carbon fixed in organic molecules by photosynthesis is eventually released back to the atmosphere through respiration and decomposition, and through combustion of fossil fuels and biomass
The oceans are solely carbon sources because dissolved CO2 is released back to the atmosphere by evaporation
explanation: Carbon cycles through photosynthesis (fixation into biomass), respiration and decomposition (release as CO2), and combustion (rapid release from long-term geological/biological storage).
